pending.body: finding_summary: `infer_workstream_order` silently fabricates a total workstream ordering when the workstream-level dependency graph is cyclic even though the bead graph is still a valid DAG. In [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:888](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:888) through [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:945](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:945), any workstreams left with nonzero indegree after the topo walk are appended in input order at lines 939-942. `render_plan_md_checked()` then publishes that sequence as the dependency-derived `Workstream order` at [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1184](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1184) and [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1308](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1308). A valid interleaved plan such as `B1 -> A1 -> A2 -> B2` creates edges `B -> A` and `A -> B`, so the rendered order becomes arbitrary and can contradict the actual sequencing constraints instead of surfacing that there is no single workstream topo order.

finding_summary: `summarize_bundle` can overstate acceptance coverage because it trusts `acceptance_map.covered_by` instead of deriving coverage from the beads or requiring full validation. `canonicalize_bundle()` only backfills `covered_by` when it is empty at [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1064](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1064) through [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1083](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1083), but `summarize_bundle()` counts every non-empty `covered_by` entry as covered at [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:953](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:953) through [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:967](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:967). That means a malformed bundle with stale or unknown `covered_by` references can report 100% acceptance coverage even when no bead actually advances those criteria.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 3 -> Plan and Implement
